name: Studynyna Rybiacha (Fish in Aspic)
recipeIngredient: 1 large firm-fleshed fish, (white fish such as Canadian sole)
recipeInstructions:
1. Wash the whole fish and fish heads in cold water. Use a knife to scrape scales from tail to head. Rinse the gut cavity.
2. Cut off the head at the gills, rinse again, and cut into equal pieces.
3. Rub salt inside and out of the fish; set aside.
4. Remove gills from the heads.
5. Combine all ingredients with 6 cups cold water in a 4-quart pot. Bring to boil, skimming foam. Reduce heat to gentle simmer and cook 20 minutes or until heads are cooked through.
6. Using a fine sieve lined with wet towel or cheesecloth, pour liquid into a 2-quart jar. Press lightly on remains; pour off remaining liquid. Place fish sections in about 2 cups stock. Bring to gentle boil and simmer 10 minutes or until fish turns white. Do not overcook.
7. Remove fish pieces with slotted spoon; place on platter to cool.
8. When cool enough to handle, remove skin and bones while keeping pieces large and intact.
9. Cover and refrigerate until cold.
10. Strain cooking liquid again and add remaining stock.
11. For stronger flavor, reheat stock slightly and reduce by a third before adding gelatin dissolved in 1 cup fish stock; taste and adjust salt if needed.
12. Pour into enough stock to cover the bottom of a 6-cup fish mold or glass pan by ½ inch. Refrigerate until set.
13. Remove peel from carrots, slice thin rounds. Arrange carrot rounds on top of aspic, add more cooled stock, and refrigerate until set.
14. Spread fish pieces over aspic, covering with additional stock; refrigerate until set.
15. When serving, invert mold onto platter (hot damp cloth helps release). Garnish with parsley sprigs and lemon slices.
